source-discipline.reason: Pattern routing is grounded in actual probe results: at least 3 distinct CN/JP probes across batches 2/3/5 established the non-EN-free-URL-discovery-fails pattern. No new claims about source existence are made; pattern routing is a procedural shortcut that respects the conservative non-VERIFIED-only constraint.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: Pattern routing is constrained to non-VERIFIED states only — preserves rule #10 existence-gate meaning. The mechanic does NOT enable new claims of gate-clearance via pattern; it only moves NULL rows into explicit-cause states.

bias_direction: Pattern routing is a yield-acceleration mechanic introduced after 5 batches of incremental yield (236 eligible vs original 221). The bias risk is toward closing out the V2-manual NULL queue with pattern-routing to artificially zero out the backlog without genuine resolution of the underlying rows. The constraint (non-VERIFIED states only) addresses the worst version of this risk — pattern routing cannot fabricate gate-clears — but it does push 6 rows into DEFERRED-V2-AUTOMATED that might (low-probability) be VERIFIABLE via portals I haven't tried. The conservative posture: future probes can upgrade pattern-routed rows; pattern routing is not a terminal state.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would object that pattern routing is exactly the kind of methodological shortcut that erodes manual verification's value. If the answer for each new CN/JP row is 'route by pattern', the manual track is functionally identical to a bulk SQL UPDATE on jurisdiction class. Counter: pattern routing is explicit and audit-trailed — every pattern-routed row's verification_note names the establishing canonicals and the routing-matrix branch. A future contributor (or me in a later session) can re-probe any pattern-routed row at any time; the routing is not destructive. By contrast, leaving rows NULL hides the routing decision entirely.
